# grainscape

![](https://github.com/achubaty/grainscape/raw/main/stickers/hexsticker.png)

## Efficient Modelling of Landscape Connectivity, Habitat, and Protected Area Networks

Given a landscape resistance surface, creates grains of connectivity and
minimum planar graph models that can be used to calculate effective
distances for landscape connectivity at multiple scales. This is a
cross-platform reimplementation and update of the `grainscape` package
(<http://grainscape.r-forge.r-project.org>).

To cite `grainscape` in publications, see `citation("grainscape")`.

### Installation

#### From CRAN

``` r

install.packages("grainscape")
```

#### From GitHub

1.  **Install development libraries:** building packages from source
    requires the appropriate development libraries for your operating
    system. See
    [here](https://support.posit.co/hc/en-us/articles/200486498-Package-Development-Prerequisites)
    for more details.

    - *Windows:* install
      [Rtools](https://cran.r-project.org/bin/windows/Rtools/).
    - *macOS:* install Xcode commandline tools from the terminal:
      `xcode-select --install`.
    - *Debian/Ubuntu Linux:* ensure `r-base-dev` is installed.

2.  **Install from GitHub:**

    ``` r

    # install.packages("remotes")
    library("remotes")
    install_github("achubaty/grainscape")
    ```

### Reporting bugs

Contact us via the package GitHub site:
<https://github.com/achubaty/grainscape/issues>.

### Contributions

This Git repository uses the [Git
Flow](https://nvie.com/posts/a-successful-git-branching-model/)
branching model (the
[`git flow`](https://github.com/petervanderdoes/gitflow-avh) extension
is useful for this). The
[`development`](https://github.com/achubaty/grainscape/tree/development)
branch contains the latest contributions and other code that will appear
in the next release, and the
[`main`](https://github.com/achubaty/grainscape) branch contains the
code of the latest release, which is exactly what is currently on
[CRAN](https://cran.r-project.org/package=grainscape).

To make a contribution to the package, just send a [pull
request](https://help.github.com/articles/using-pull-requests/). When
you send your PR, make sure `development` is the destination branch on
the [grainscape repository](https://github.com/achubaty/grainscape).
Your PR should pass `R CMD check --as-cran`, which will also be checked
by [GitHub Actions](https://github.com/achubaty/grainscape/actions) when
the PR is submitted.

See
[`CONTRIBUTING.md`](https://github.com/achubaty/grainscape/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)
for more details.

### Code of Conduct

Please note that the grainscape project is released with a [Contributor
Code of
Conduct](https://www.alexchubaty.com/grainscape/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.html).
By contributing to this project, you agree to abide by its terms.

# Package index

## All functions

- [`GOC()`](https://www.alexchubaty.com/grainscape/reference/GOC.md) :
  Produce a grains of connectivity model at multiple scales (patch-based
  or lattice GOC)

- [`MPG()`](https://www.alexchubaty.com/grainscape/reference/MPG.md) :
  Extract a minimum planar graph (MPG) model from a landscape resistance
  surface

- [`corridor-class`](https://www.alexchubaty.com/grainscape/reference/corridor-class.md)
  :

  The `corridor` class

- [`corridor()`](https://www.alexchubaty.com/grainscape/reference/corridor.md)
  : Visualize corridors between two points using a grains of
  connectivity (GOC)

- [`distance()`](https://www.alexchubaty.com/grainscape/reference/distance.md)
  : Find the grains of connectivity network distance

- [`export()`](https://www.alexchubaty.com/grainscape/reference/export.md)
  : Export spatial data from MPG and GOC models

- [`` `$`( ``*`<goc>`*`)`](https://www.alexchubaty.com/grainscape/reference/extract.md)
  [`` `$<-`( ``*`<goc>`*`)`](https://www.alexchubaty.com/grainscape/reference/extract.md)
  [`` `$`( ``*`<mpg>`*`)`](https://www.alexchubaty.com/grainscape/reference/extract.md)
  [`` `$<-`( ``*`<mpg>`*`)`](https://www.alexchubaty.com/grainscape/reference/extract.md)
  : Extract or Replace Parts of an Object

- [`ggGS()`](https://www.alexchubaty.com/grainscape/reference/ggGS.md) :

  Prepare data in `MPG` and `grain` objects for use with ggplot2

- [`goc-class`](https://www.alexchubaty.com/grainscape/reference/goc-class.md)
  :

  The `goc` class

- [`grain-class`](https://www.alexchubaty.com/grainscape/reference/grain-class.md)
  :

  The `grain` class

- [`grain()`](https://www.alexchubaty.com/grainscape/reference/grain.md)
  : Extract a grain of connectivity (GOC) tessellation at a given scale

- [`grainscape-maps`](https://www.alexchubaty.com/grainscape/reference/grainscape-maps.md)
  :

  Test maps included with `grainscape`

- [`grainscape`](https://www.alexchubaty.com/grainscape/reference/grainscape-package.md)
  [`grainscape-package`](https://www.alexchubaty.com/grainscape/reference/grainscape-package.md)
  : Landscape Connectivity, Habitat, and Protected Area Networks

- [`graphdf()`](https://www.alexchubaty.com/grainscape/reference/graphdf.md)
  :

  Produce a `data.frame` containing the structure and associated
  attributes

- [`mpg-class`](https://www.alexchubaty.com/grainscape/reference/mpg-class.md)
  :

  The `mpg` class

- [`patchFilter()`](https://www.alexchubaty.com/grainscape/reference/patchFilter.md)
  : Filter out patches smaller than a specified area

- [`plot(`*`<corridor>`*`,`*`<ANY>`*`)`](https://www.alexchubaty.com/grainscape/reference/plot.md)
  [`plot(`*`<grain>`*`,`*`<ANY>`*`)`](https://www.alexchubaty.com/grainscape/reference/plot.md)
  [`plot(`*`<mpg>`*`,`*`<ANY>`*`)`](https://www.alexchubaty.com/grainscape/reference/plot.md)
  :

  Plot quick visualizations of `grainscape` objects

- [`plotResistance()`](https://www.alexchubaty.com/grainscape/reference/plotResistance.md)
  : Plot a resistance surface with a consistent colour scheme

- [`plotWithResistance()`](https://www.alexchubaty.com/grainscape/reference/plotWithResistance.md)
  : Show a resistance surface beside a network plot

- [`point()`](https://www.alexchubaty.com/grainscape/reference/point.md)
  : Identify the polygons containing locations in grains of connectivity
  (GOC) tessellations

- [`show(`*`<goc>`*`)`](https://www.alexchubaty.com/grainscape/reference/show.md)
  [`show(`*`<grain>`*`)`](https://www.alexchubaty.com/grainscape/reference/show.md)
  [`show(`*`<corridor>`*`)`](https://www.alexchubaty.com/grainscape/reference/show.md)
  :

  Show a `grainscape` object

- [`theme_grainscape()`](https://www.alexchubaty.com/grainscape/reference/theme_grainscape.md)
  :

  A `ggplot2` theme for `grainscape`

- [`threshold()`](https://www.alexchubaty.com/grainscape/reference/threshold.md)
  : Produce a minimum planar graph (MPG) at multiple scales

### All vignettes

  package](https://www.alexchubaty.com/grainscape/articles/grainscape_vignette.md):
- [Calculating the minimum planar
  graph](https://www.alexchubaty.com/grainscape/articles/MPG_algorithm.md):
